name: LLM via Executa
description: Exposes a `complete` tool that wraps host sampling/createMessage via reverse-RPC, letting the LLM Demo app run completions through an Executa instead of calling anna.llm.complete directly.
_comment: Publish metadata (slug/name/version/executa_type) is read by `anna-app apps publish`; the dev fields (tool_id/type/enabled) are read by `anna-app dev`. The `distribution` block now uses the switchable multi-profile form: `profiles` declares `local` and `binary` side by side, and `active` selects which one `anna-app apps/executa publish` forwards to the server. Flip `active` to `local` to publish the dev-path locator, or back to `binary` to ship the self-contained PyInstaller binary per platform from this repo's GitHub Release (tag `llm-via-executa-v<version>`), built by .github/workflows/anna-app-publish.yml; on `anna-app apps cut` matrix-nexus mirrors the bytes into apps.anna.partners R2 and pins sha256 (so no self-hosting / no sha256 needed here). The flat single-mode form (`distribution.type` + sibling fields) is still accepted. This dir is the `llm-via-executa` handle referenced by app.json#bundled_executas. Disable here if you want to test the app without the Executa-LLM path.
